title: Football
questions: Do you prefer to watch of play football?
Who is your favourite team?
Why do you support them?
Who are your team's biggest rivals?
Do you go to many live matches?
Who is your favourite player? Why?
Who do you think the greatest footballer in the world is? What makes him great?
What do you love about football? Why?
What position would you play? Why?
Which match was your most memorable? Why?
What do you think of your national team? Why?
What do you know about Pele?
Have you ever been to a World Cup match?
Do you play for a football team now?
Do you play football games on the computer?
Do you think footballers are paid too much? Why?
Which league is the strongest in the world? Why?
Which is the best team in the world? Why?
Why, in your opinion, is football (also known as soccer) so popular? What is the point of kicking a ball around for an hour and a half? Is football a waste of time?
Do you think footballers are paid too much? Do they receive too much attention from the media?

How responsible should football players be to their fans? Do younger fans try to emulate football celebrities? Should popular players be seen to behave well both on and off the pitch in order to set an example to impressionable fans?
How should players be dealt with for bad behaviour such as spitting at, or kicking another player maliciously during a match?
Why does football have hooligans when other sports such as cricket, rugby and American football don't? What can be done to reduce football hooliganism?

Children often want to emulate their football heroes to the point of wanting to wear their team's 'kit'. Is it right that football clubs have taken to changing their kit every season, thus obliging parents to buy new kit for their kids every few months?
